a_image.alt: Boarding Pass UI style mood board — Skeuomorphic · Travel

sections.a_description: Bold destination codes, perforated edges, and the structured urgency of travel information — Boarding Pass UI mimics the distinctive card design of airline tickets with their strict columnar layouts, monospaced flight codes, and that satisfying tear-along-the-dotted-line perforation. It transforms the screen into a physical travel document, complete with airline-blue accents and utilitarian condensed type.

This aesthetic appears on travel booking platforms, airline loyalty program sites, and tourism startup landing pages. You'll find it on event ticketing apps, travel blog headers, transit system interfaces, and any project that wants to channel the structured excitement of departure — that moment when you hold your boarding pass and the journey begins.

a_image_cover.alt: Boarding Pass UI style use case illustration
Boarding Pass UI style use case illustration
Boarding Pass UI style use case illustration

sections.a_description: A physical boarding pass — the distinctive perforated card with bold destination codes, flight information in strict columns, and that airline-ticket materiality. Background: warm light gray (#e8e4de) simulating an airport counter surface. The .inner panel is the boarding pass itself: white (#ffffff) background with a dotted-line perforation along the right side (border-right: 2px dashed #ccc), and a colored accent band at the top (airline blue #1565c0). The layout feels structured and information-dense. Typography is utilitarian and airline-like. Set in Roboto Condensed for headings and labels — its condensed proportions channel the space-efficient typography of airline tickets and luggage tags. Roboto Mono for the eyebrow — monospaced type mimicking flight codes and gate numbers. Heading in Roboto Condensed at weight 700, 28px, uppercase, 2px letter-spacing, color #1565c0. Eyebrow in Roboto Mono at 12px, weight 400, uppercase, 3px letter-spacing, color #888. Body at 14px Roboto Condensed, weight 400, line-height 1.6, color #333. Buttons: primary — airline blue (#1565c0), white text, 4px border-radius. Secondary: transparent, blue border. Alternatives: Barlow Condensed, Oswald, IBM Plex Sans Condensed.
